findings.evidence: `Trivial.lean:214` defines `trivial` as `groupLike ... (1 : GroupLike R C)`, while `MatrixCoefficientTrivial.lean:63`, `:73`, `:92`, `:116`, and `:127` already prove the corresponding group-like statements.
findings.fix: Make the trivial lemmas one-line specializations of `groupLike_matrixCoefficientSubmodule_le_span_singleton`, `groupLike_rankOne_matrixCoefficientSubmodule_eq_span_singleton`, and `groupLike_matrixCoefficientSubalgebra_le_adjoin_singleton`, adding/using the bottom-vs-`adjoin {1}` fact for the subalgebra statements.
findings.issue: The trivial lemmas at lines 163-207 duplicate the just-proved group-like coefficient/span/adjoin arguments instead of specializing them to `g = 1`.

summary: The statements are at the right conceptual level, but the trivial block re-proves special cases after introducing the general group-like results. Derive the trivial API from the group-like API so the specialization through `1 : GroupLike R C` is the single maintained path.
